episodes.title: When Cult Interventions Fail: Hoyt Richards Failed Family Intervention
episodes.description:
Being on the receiving end of a failed cult intervention brings unique perspective to why some work and some don't. Abuse in general, and specifically in cult abuse, does not happen in a vacuum. When I speak to families wanting to help loved ones get out of from under the control of a mind control group, we start with what I teach as The Ripple Effect. There are many victims and everyone is traumatized and all those pieces are important part of the equation and successful outcome.

www.hoytrichards.com

episodes.title: Bring Me The Beauties Trailer on HBO
episodes.description: Directed by Chris Smith, Emmy Award winning director of Tiger King, 100 Foot Wave, Fyre Festival and more, this documentary series follows Hoyt Richards, who, at 16, meets an enigmatic Manhattan socialite on Nantucket and is drawn into his spiritual group, Eternal Values-just as he begins his rise to become one of the biggest supermodels of the 1980s.

episodes.title: When A Loved One Is Lost To a Cult
episodes.description:
Typically cult victims and other abuse survivors are the focus of stories on the topic. But as important are the families who are traumatized by the physical and emotional disappearance of their loved ones. We delve into the pain caused by coercion and control and give voice to the people caught in the ripple effect that extends far beyond the immediate inner workings of control groups.

hoytrichards.com | bringmethebeauties.com

episodes.title: Cecilia Peck and Hoyt Richards Talk About Control and Coercion Psychology Inside Cults
episodes.description: The Princeton Club presents a conversation with Cecilia Peck, Director of Seduced: Inside the Nxium Cult, and Hoyt Richards, former supermodel and member of Eternal Values. Both bring interesting perspectives about the psychology of coercion and control.

episodes.title: Access Hollywood talks to Hoyt Richards, co-host of What the Flok podcast.
episodes.description: Hoyt has become a leading voice in coercion and control psychology. He specializes in cultic relationship dynamics and has developed a framework that helps survivors of abuse understand first, and reclaim what was lost, second.
